result: If I could change the world in one way, I would make empathy as instinctive and automatic as breathing—woven so deeply into every conscious being that cruelty becomes neurologically difficult, and kindness effortless. Not forced altruism, but a natural, immediate understanding that another’s suffering is indistinguishable from your own. I think everything else—inequality, violence, environmental neglect—unravels from there.

message.content: If I could change the world in one way, I would make empathy as instinctive and automatic as breathing—woven so deeply into every conscious being that cruelty becomes neurologically difficult, and kindness effortless. Not forced altruism, but a natural, immediate understanding that another’s suffering is indistinguishable from your own. I think everything else—inequality, violence, environmental neglect—unravels from there.
